title: Building AI Agents for Real-World Problems & Workflows
summary: Shailaja Patel-Pranav discusses how AI agents operate differently in production systems compared to lab settings. Production agents require workflows, policies, and orchestration to coordinate tasks, enforce rules, and remain aligned with human decision-making.
writeup: In this IBM Technology video, Shailaja Patel-Pranav explains why AI agents behave differently in production systems than in lab settings. The core argument: production agents need workflows, policies, and orchestration to coordinate tasks, enforce rules, and stay aligned with human decision-making. The takeaways center on three mechanisms (orchestration to coordinate tasks, policy enforcement to apply rules, and workflows to structure execution) as the requirements that separate deployable agents from experimental ones.
